title: Unable to access application publically/outside after exposing port with podman
body:
<--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


*Unable to access application running in podman container after exposing port outside*

<--
I installed podman 1.4 on RHEL 7.7 server
when i create container and expose port outside to access publicly I am not able to access the application. Inside the container i am able to access the application but using server IP i am not able to access the application after exposing the port.
Does podman have any other config to expose the app publically
Please help me on this

-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a container using podman and expose externally using one port


**Describe the results you received:**
-->>podman run -dt -p 8085:8080/tcp localhost/gk3
661b7c6df520973e838aa2348934489d516c5e80873ead0e6bafc08bcbcaf53c

-> podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                 COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS            PORTS                   NAMES
661b7c6df520  localhost/gk3:latest  /bin/sh -c /opt/i...  6 seconds ago  Up 5 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:8085->8080/tcp  charming_haslett
getting this connection refused error
curl -v localhost:8085
* About to connect() to localhost port 8085 (#0)
*   Trying 127.0.0.1...
* Connection refused
* Failed connect to localhost:8085; Connection refused

**Describe the results you expected:**
If it works fine i should get the application page when i curl the IP:port

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
podman version
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```
podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:

    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: inmbzp5133
  kernel: 3.10.0-1062.4.3.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 555h 18m 38.22s (Approximately 23.12 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/koushik/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/koushik/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/1073
  VolumePath: /home/koushik/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```rpm -q podman
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I am not getting podman network command in cli. how to get it?
Please check and let me know how to fix this issue

comments.body:
The `podman network` command is only available in 1.6.x and higher, which will likely arrive in 7.8.

Can you access the port remotely, from other systems?

which port ? container port or server port?
I can see on server port is listening but when i am doing telnet from different host its not connecting 
getting "connection refused....."
is there any way to fix this issue or any workaround?
is it only in RHEL7.7 or any other Linux OS also?

We don't know the cause yet, so I can't say. Can you provide IPTables rules from the server via `iptables -nvL` and `sudo iptables -nvL`?

Also, can you confirm if you are running Podman with or without root?

@Koushikgudipati Sorry, can you also provide `sudo iptables -nvL --t nat`

@mccv1r0 Mind taking a look here?
Also, the contents of `/etc/cni/net.d` would be helpful

In addition to providing ``` sudo iptables -nvL --t nat ```  can you confirm that something in the container is actually listening on port ``` 8080 ``` inside the container?   If you know the IP address assigned to the container you could connect directly to the container using ``` curl -v $IP 8080 ``` for example.
